UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile in the Sunshine application, including their financial balance and other personal settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, which is always 'user'.

saldoUSDT.description: The user's current investment balance in USDT (Tether). This field is read-only for users and can only be modified through approved deposit requests.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface (e.g., 'es' for Spanish, 'en' for English).

termsAccepted.description: Indicates whether the user has accepted the 'Términos y Condiciones de Inversión de Sunshine' during registration.

invitationCodeUsedId.description: Reference to the InvitationCode entity that was used by this user during registration. (Relationship: InvitationCode 1:N UserProfile)

ultimoCheckIn.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of the user's last daily check-in.

walletAddress.description: The user's unique USDT (TRC-20) wallet address, used to prevent multi-accounting.

planActivo.description: The amount of the user's active investment plan in USDT. 0 if no active plan.

inversionAnterior.description: Stores the value of the previous investment to calculate commissions on the difference when a plan is upgraded.

fechaInicioPlan.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of when the user's active investment plan started. Can be null.

bonoDirecto.description: The accumulated amount of direct referral bonuses in USDT.

bonoEntregado.description: Indica el estado del bono de bienvenida: false (inactivo), true (listo para reclamar), 'reclamado' (pagado).

fechaRegistro.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of when the user account was created.

InvitationCode.title: InvitationCode
InvitationCode.description: Represents an invitation code required for new user registration in the Sunshine application. Each code can be used only once.

id.description: The unique invitation code string itself, serving as its identifier (e.g., 'SUNSHINE2023').

isUsed.description: Indicates whether this invitation code has been used to register a user.

usedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that used this invitation code. Only present if 'isUsed' is true. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 InvitationCode)

createdAt.description: The date and time when this invitation code was created.

WalletAddress.title: WalletAddress
WalletAddress.description: Represents a unique wallet address to prevent multi-accounting. The document ID is the wallet address itself.

userId.description: The UID of the user this wallet belongs to.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the wallet was registered.

InviteCodeMap.title: InviteCodeMap
InviteCodeMap.description: Maps a user's unique invite code to their user ID for efficient and secure sponsor lookup during registration.

userId.description: The UID of the user who owns this invite code.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document ID (`userId`) corresponds to a Firebase Authentication UID. 'saldoUSDT' is read-only for users.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID.

definition.description: Stores unique invitation codes. Unauthenticated users can perform a `get()` to verify a specific code during registration.

params.name: invitationCodeId
params.description: The unique invitation code string itself.

definition.description: Stores unique wallet addresses to prevent multi-accounting. Used during registration to check for uniqueness. Document ID is the wallet address.
Stores a mapping from a user's public invite code to their UID. Allows for unauthenticated sponsor lookups without exposing the main users collection.

params.name: inviteCodeId
params.description: The user's unique invite code string.

firestore.reasoning: The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization. Key collections like `wallet_addresses` and `invite_codes_map` are separated to allow for safe, unauthenticated checks during registration without exposing the primary `users` collection. This design prevents security rule complexity and avoids slow `get()` calls within rules.
